code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Complete functional failure despite proper usage attempts
    - Multi-purchase defects overriding any initial satisfaction
    - Safety hazards with evidence of danger
    - Usability failures making concept irrelevant

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Persistent frustration despite partial functionality
    - Requires constant workarounds for basic operation
    - Inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - Explicit "regret purchase" declarations

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Adequate for specific scenarios only
    - Technical competence with notable compromises
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Usage limitations affecting functionality ("don't overstuff")
    - Requires break-in period for full performance
    - Assembly difficulties without functional impact
    - Factual praise lacking enthusiasm ("does the job")

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Close comparisons to top brands with satisfaction
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Concise functional praise without negatives
    - 3+ complimented aspects (durability/design/utility)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH core functional excellence
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite minor imperfections

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Nearly matches X" comparisons →5 if satisfied
    - Functional praise + usage limit →4 / + non-functional →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Core functionality achieved post-setup →5 if flawless
    - Brief effectiveness praise →5 even if concise
    - Material maintenance needs →5 if non-functional
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example1:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Favorable brand comparison with functional satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed - deep pockets fit any mattress"
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise without negatives →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example3:
Review: "Sharp tablecloth. Only con is inherent need to iron cotton"
<Reasoning>Non-functional criticism with core excellence →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example4:
Review: "Stasher bag perfect for snacks. Don't overstuff - affects seal"
<Reasoning>Functional limitation impacts core utility →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example5:
Review: "Solved puppy+kid messes better than any previous cleaner!"
<Reasoning>Solves persistent problem exceeding expectations →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example6:
Review: "Good storage bin - not extraordinary but keeps items organized"
<Reasoning>Functional adequacy without enthusiasm →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example7:
Review: "Rollers handle bumps perfectly! Installation took muscle"
<Reasoning>Core excellence outweighs setup effort →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example8:
Review: "Constantly retighten screws - not worth the effort"
<Reasoning>Excessive maintenance for basic function →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example9:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous electrical defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety hazards →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example10:
Review: "Thin material concerns but adequate for light use"
<Reasoning>Technical competence with notable compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (31 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 7
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 3: 1
  gold 5 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This mattress get 4 stars for comfort, but there's so many other nice features about it that it gets 5 starts for everything else. To begin with, I wouldn't suggest using this as a long term every day bed for someone. The shallow design and budget pr..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "It's a very solid bed that feels like it's going to hold up to just about anything. Assembly wasn't too difficult, though there were a couple of parts that had to be under some pressure to get the screw holes to line up just right. Also, when we firs..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "The color pattern here is very pleasant. The rug looks great. I wish I had tiled my whole floor like this. The non-stick feature is sprayed on rather that woven into the rug. I hope that it holds up."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  5. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "When it comes to cookware cast iron is about as durable as it gets. Yes, it can be a bit harder to take care of (don't throw it in the dishwasher and towel dry it after washing) but it will literally last generations. This skillet came pre-seasoned w..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This machine seems determined to foil every effort I make to clean my carpets. The bottom section never works. It's job is to collect the dirty water, but since the top section hardly puts any water out at all, it doesn't have much to do. I have labo..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 2, 1] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 31 errors, 20 borderline out of 80 examples."}
